disposal_methods.label: Bulk Waste (sodai gomi)
steps: Apply online or call your city's bulk waste center
Buy a sodai gomi sticker at a konbini
Attach sticker and place at designated spot on collection day
disposal_methods.note: Cost varies by size. 2-seater around 800 yen, 3-seater+ up to 2800 yen in Tokyo wards.

disposal_methods.label: Sell Second-Hand
disposal_methods.note: Jimoty is best for large furniture — buyer picks up for free.

disposal_methods.label: Paid Pickup Service
disposal_methods.note: Private haulers offer same-day pickup. Confirm licensing (一般廃棄物収集運搬許可).

disposal_methods.label: Home Appliance Recycling Law
steps: Pay recycling fee at a post office (yuubin kyoku)
Arrange collection via retailer or designated collection point
Keep the recycling ticket receipt
disposal_methods.note: Recycling fee: 3740 yen (170L or smaller) to 4730 yen (171L+). Collection/transport fee ~1000-3000 yen extra.

disposal_methods.label: Sell Second-Hand
disposal_methods.note: Shops may buy fridges under 5 years old. Older units are usually refused.

disposal_methods.label: Home Appliance Recycling Law
steps: Pay recycling fee at a post office
Arrange collection via retailer or designated collection point
Keep the recycling ticket receipt
disposal_methods.note: Recycling fee: 2530 yen. Clothes dryers also 2530 yen. Transport fee ~1000-3000 yen extra.

disposal_methods.label: Sell Second-Hand
disposal_methods.note: Machines under 5 years old in working condition may be purchased.

disposal_methods.label: Home Appliance Recycling Law
steps: Pay recycling fee at a post office
Arrange collection via retailer or designated collection point
Keep the recycling ticket receipt
disposal_methods.note: Recycling fee: 1320-1870 yen (15 inch and under), 2420-3700 yen (16 inch+). Varies by manufacturer.

disposal_methods.label: Sell Second-Hand
disposal_methods.note: Smart TVs and recent models sell well. CRT TVs are very difficult to sell.

disposal_methods.label: Home Appliance Recycling Law
steps: Pay recycling fee at a post office
Hire a licensed technician to remove the unit
Arrange collection via retailer or designated point
disposal_methods.note: Recycling fee: 990-2000 yen. Removal by technician costs 4000-8000 yen extra. Never attempt DIY removal — refrigerant gas is regulated.

disposal_methods.label: Bulk Waste (sodai gomi)
steps: Apply online or call your city's bulk waste center
Buy a sodai gomi sticker at a konbini
Remove the防犯登録 (bouhan touroku / bike registration) at a police box or bike shop before disposal
Attach sticker and place at designated spot
disposal_methods.note: Most Tokyo wards charge 400-800 yen. You must cancel your bike registration first.

disposal_methods.label: Sell Second-Hand
disposal_methods.note: Working bikes sell easily on Jimoty for local pickup.

disposal_methods.label: Manufacturer Recycling (free for post-2003 PCs)
steps: Check for the PC Recycling Mark sticker on your device
Apply to the manufacturer's recycling program online
Receive a prepaid shipping label and send the device
disposal_methods.note: PCs with the PC Recycling Mark (PCリサイクルマーク) are collected free. Without the mark: ~3300 yen (desktop/laptop) or ~4400 yen (with monitor).

disposal_methods.label: Sell Second-Hand
disposal_methods.note: Wipe all data before selling. Working laptops sell well on Mercari.

disposal_methods.label: Bulk Waste (sodai gomi)
steps: Apply online or call your city's bulk waste center
Buy a sodai gomi sticker at a konbini
Attach sticker and place at designated spot on collection day
disposal_methods.note: Small desks ~400 yen, large office desks up to 2800 yen.

disposal_methods.label: Sell Second-Hand
disposal_methods.note: Standing desks and IKEA desks sell quickly on Jimoty.

disposal_methods.label: Bulk Waste (sodai gomi)
steps: Apply online or call your city's bulk waste center
Buy a sodai gomi sticker at a konbini
Attach sticker and place at designated spot on collection day
disposal_methods.note: 400 yen for small units, up to 2000 yen for tall/wide bookshelves.

disposal_methods.label: Sell Second-Hand
disposal_methods.note: Heavy shelves are hard to ship. Jimoty local pickup is ideal.

disposal_methods.label: Bulk Waste (sodai gomi)
steps: Apply online or call your city's bulk waste center
Buy a sodai gomi sticker at a konbini
Attach sticker and place at designated spot on collection day
disposal_methods.note: Mattress (single): ~800 yen, mattress (double): ~1200 yen, bed frame: ~1200-2800 yen. Spring mattresses may require separate disposal.

disposal_methods.label: Paid Pickup Service
disposal_methods.note: Large beds are difficult to move. Private haulers can carry them downstairs.

disposal_methods.label: Bulk Waste (sodai gomi)
steps: Apply online or call your city's bulk waste center
Buy a sodai gomi sticker at a konbini
Fold or roll the futon and tie it with string
Attach sticker and place at designated spot
disposal_methods.note: Usually 400 yen per futon in Tokyo wards.

disposal_methods.label: Combustible Trash (if cut up)
steps: Cut the futon into pieces small enough to fit in a designated trash bag
Place in combustible trash on collection day
disposal_methods.note: If you can cut it to fit in a 45L trash bag, it goes out as regular combustible trash for free.

disposal_methods.label: Bulk Waste (sodai gomi)
steps: Apply online or call your city's bulk waste center
Buy a sodai gomi sticker at a konbini
Attach sticker and place at designated spot on collection day
disposal_methods.note: Most wards charge 400-800 yen. Microwaves are NOT covered by the Home Appliance Recycling Law.

disposal_methods.label: Sell Second-Hand
disposal_methods.note: Working microwaves under 5 years old sell easily.

disposal_methods.label: Non-combustible Trash
steps: Check your city's collection schedule for non-combustible trash
Place in the designated collection area on the correct day
disposal_methods.note: Small rice cookers (under 30cm) usually go out as non-combustible trash for free.

disposal_methods.label: Bulk Waste (if over 30cm)
steps: Apply online or call your city's bulk waste center
Buy a sodai gomi sticker at a konbini
Attach sticker and place at designated spot
disposal_methods.note: Large rice cookers (over 30cm in any dimension) count as bulk waste.

disposal_methods.label: Sell Second-Hand
disposal_methods.note: Japanese rice cookers (Zojirushi, Tiger) are popular with expats.

disposal_methods.label: Bulk Waste (sodai gomi)
steps: Apply online or call your city's bulk waste center
Buy a sodai gomi sticker at a konbini
Attach sticker and place at designated spot on collection day
disposal_methods.note: Small table ~400 yen, dining table ~1000-2800 yen.

disposal_methods.label: Sell Second-Hand
disposal_methods.note: Kotatsu tables are seasonal — sell in autumn for best price.

disposal_methods.label: Bulk Waste (sodai gomi)
steps: Apply online or call your city's bulk waste center
Buy a sodai gomi sticker at a konbini
Attach sticker and place at designated spot on collection day
disposal_methods.note: Regular chair ~400 yen, office chair ~800 yen in most Tokyo wards.

disposal_methods.label: Sell Second-Hand
disposal_methods.note: Gaming chairs and Herman Miller chairs sell well.

disposal_methods.label: Bulk Waste (sodai gomi)
steps: Remove ink cartridges (return to manufacturer or recycle at electronics store)
Apply online or call your city's bulk waste center
Buy a sodai gomi sticker at a konbini
Attach sticker and place at designated spot
disposal_methods.note: 400-800 yen. Return ink cartridges separately at Bic Camera, Yodobashi, etc.

disposal_methods.label: Sell Second-Hand
disposal_methods.note: Only recent models with working printheads have resale value.

disposal_methods.label: Bulk Waste (sodai gomi)
steps: Apply online or call your city's bulk waste center
Buy a sodai gomi sticker at a konbini
Attach sticker and place at designated spot on collection day
disposal_methods.note: Usually 400 yen. Handheld/stick vacuums under 30cm may go as non-combustible trash.

disposal_methods.label: Sell Second-Hand
disposal_methods.note: Dyson and Roomba models retain good resale value.

disposal_methods.label: Bulk Waste (sodai gomi)
steps: Apply online or call your city's bulk waste center
Buy a sodai gomi sticker at a konbini
Attach sticker and place at designated spot on collection day
disposal_methods.note: 400 yen for most fans. Small desk fans under 30cm may go as non-combustible trash.

disposal_methods.label: Sell Second-Hand
disposal_methods.note: Sell in spring/early summer for best price.

disposal_methods.label: Bulk Waste (sodai gomi)
steps: Drain all kerosene from oil heaters completely
Apply online or call your city's bulk waste center
Buy a sodai gomi sticker at a konbini
Attach sticker and place at designated spot on collection day
disposal_methods.note: 400-800 yen. IMPORTANT: Kerosene heaters must be fully drained. Some wards refuse heaters with fuel.

disposal_methods.label: Sell Second-Hand
disposal_methods.note: Sell in autumn for best price. Kerosene heaters are harder to sell secondhand.

disposal_methods.label: Combustible Trash
steps: Place in a transparent or semi-transparent bag
Put out on combustible trash collection day
disposal_methods.note: Clothes go out as regular combustible trash for free in most wards.

disposal_methods.label: Clothing Recycling Box
steps: Find a clothing recycling box at your ward office or community center
Place clean, dry clothes in the box
disposal_methods.note: Many wards have free collection events or permanent drop-off boxes.

disposal_methods.label: Sell Second-Hand
disposal_methods.note: Brand-name items sell well on Mercari. 2nd Street and Book Off buy clothes in bulk.
